title: Delete dead SQLite schema files, drizzle config, and migration directory
consumer_purpose: Remove dead SQLite code from opencode package — none of these files are imported by runtime code, the barrel file (schema.ts) exports from .pg.sql.ts equivalents, and package.json scripts reference drizzle.config.pg.ts
claim_atoms: 1. All 8 SQLite schema files are dead — no imports reference them 2. drizzle.config.ts is dead — package.json scripts use drizzle.config.pg.ts 3. migration/ directory is dead — PG migrations live in migration-pg/ 4. Deleting these won't break typecheck or tests
content:
## Plan: SQLite dead code removal (Lane SQL-001)

### Phase 1: Delete 8 SQLite Drizzle schema files
Files to delete (confirmed no imports from runtime code):
- `src/session/session.sql.ts`
- `src/account/account.sql.ts`
- `src/project/project.sql.ts`
- `src/share/share.sql.ts`
- `src/event/event.sql.ts`
- `src/control-plane/workspace.sql.ts`
- `src/sync/event.sql.ts`
- `src/data-migration.sql.ts`

### Phase 2: Delete SQLite drizzle config
- `drizzle.config.ts` (PG config at `drizzle.config.pg.ts` is the active one)

### Phase 3: Delete SQLite migration directory
- `migration/` directory (20 subdirectories) — PG migrations live in `migration-pg/`

### Phase 4: Verify
- Run `bun typecheck` from packages/opencode — expect no new errors
- Run `bun test test/storage/migration.pg.test.ts` — PG migration tests should pass
- Run `bun test test/storage/migration.duckdb.test.ts` — DuckDB tests should pass
- Confirm git diff shows only the deleted files

### Risks
- Low risk: No runtime code imports these files
- Low risk: Barrel file (schema.ts) re-exports from .pg.sql.ts versions
- Low risk: package.json scripts explicitly reference drizzle.config.pg.ts
- Dirty file risk: None — these files are not modified in the working tree per git status
